info.title: ActivitySmith API
info.description: Send push notifications and Live Activities to your own devices via a single API key.

tags.name: PushNotifications
tags.description: Send push notifications to paired devices.

tags.name: LiveActivities
tags.description: Start, update, stream, and end Live Activities.

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: Update metric values shown in ActivitySmith widgets.

post.description: Sends a push notification to devices matched by API key scope and optional target channels. Supports optional redirection URL, optional media preview or playback when the notification is expanded, and up to 4 interactive actions. `media` cannot be combined with `actions`.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import os
from activitysmith import ActivitySmith

activitysmith = ActivitySmith(api_key=os.environ["ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"])

activitysmith.notifications.send({
    "title": "New subscription 💸",
    "message": "Customer upgraded to Pro plan",
})

x-codeSamples.label: Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->notifications->send([
    'title' => 'New subscription 💸',
    'message' => 'Customer upgraded to Pro plan',
]);

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

activitysmith.notifications.send(
  title: "New subscription 💸",
  message: "Customer upgraded to Pro plan"
)

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://activitysmith.com/api/push-notification" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "New subscription 💸",
    "message": "Customer upgraded to Pro plan"
  }'

value.title: New subscription 💸
value.message: Customer upgraded to Pro plan

400.description: Bad request (invalid payload or channel targeting input)

value.message: target.channels must be an array of channel slugs

value.message: This API key can only target assigned channels. Requested channels include channels outside this key scope.

put.summary: Start a new Live Activity or update an existing one
put.description: Use a stable stream_key for each ongoing thing you want to show as a Live Activity. Send the latest content_state whenever it changes, and ActivitySmith will keep the Live Activity in sync. For timer streams, send duration_seconds to start or reset the timer; omit duration_seconds on later updates to preserve the existing timer window. Use secondary_action for a second button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities only.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->liveActivities->stream('search-reindex', [
    'content_state' => [
        'title' => 'Search Reindex',
        'subtitle' => 'catalog-v2',
        'type' => 'progress',
        'percentage' => 42,
    ],
]);

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

activitysmith.live_activities.stream(
  "search-reindex",
  content_state: {
    title: "Search Reindex",
    subtitle: "catalog-v2",
    type: "progress",
    percentage: 42
  }
)

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

parameters.name: stream_key
parameters.description: Stable identifier for one ongoing thing. Allowed characters: letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens.

content_state.title: Approval Needed
content_state.message: Should I send the follow-up email to Brightlane?

400.description: Bad request (invalid stream_key, payload, action, or channel targeting input)

value.message: stream_key must contain only letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens
channels must contain at least one channel

403.description: Forbidden (API key scope or channel assignment violation)

value.message: This API key can only target assigned channels. Requested channels include channels outside this key scope.

404.description: No recipients found for effective channel target

value.message: No Live Activity devices matched the effective channel target
Too many requests, please try again later.
Only 4 concurrent Live Activities are allowed per account

delete.description: Use this endpoint when the process you are tracking is finished and you no longer want the Live Activity on your devices. ActivitySmith ends the current Live Activity for this stream and dismisses it from devices. If you need direct lifecycle control, use /live-activity/start, /live-activity/update, and /live-activity/end instead. Use secondary_action for a second button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities only.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->liveActivities->endStream('prod-web-1', [
    'content_state' => [
        'title' => 'Server Health',
        'subtitle' => 'prod-web-1',
        'type' => 'metrics',
        'metrics' => [
            ['label' => 'CPU', 'value' => 7, 'unit' => '%'],
            ['label' => 'MEM', 'value' => 38, 'unit' => '%'],
        ],
    ],
]);

cURL

post.summary: Start a Live Activity (legacy manual lifecycle)
post.description: Legacy manual lifecycle endpoint. For new integrations, use PUT /live-activity/stream/{stream_key} so ActivitySmith can manage start, update, rotation, and end state for you. This endpoint remains supported for existing integrations and advanced lifecycle control. Starts a Live Activity on devices matched by API key scope and optional target channels. Supports segmented_progress, progress, metrics, stats, alert, and timer activity types. For segmented_progress activities, number_of_steps can be changed later during update or end calls if the workflow changes. Use secondary_action for a second button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities only.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import os
from activitysmith import ActivitySmith

activitysmith = ActivitySmith(api_key=os.environ["ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"])

start = activitysmith.live_activities.start({
    "content_state": {
        "title": "Nightly database backup",
        "subtitle": "create snapshot",
        "number_of_steps": 3,
        "current_step": 1,
        "type": "segmented_progress",
        "color": "yellow",
    },
})

activity_id = start.activity_id

x-codeSamples.label: Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$start = $activitysmith->liveActivities->start([
    'content_state' => [
        'title' => 'Nightly database backup',
        'subtitle' => 'create snapshot',
        'number_of_steps' => 3,
        'current_step' => 1,
        'type' => 'segmented_progress',
        'color' => 'yellow',
    ],
]);

$activityId = $start->getActivityId();

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

start = activitysmith.live_activities.start(
  content_state: {
    title: "Nightly database backup",
    subtitle: "create snapshot",
    number_of_steps: 3,
    current_step: 1,
    type: "segmented_progress",
    color: "yellow"
  }
)

activity_id = start.activity_id

post.summary: Update a Live Activity (legacy manual lifecycle)
post.description: Legacy manual lifecycle endpoint. For new integrations, use PUT /live-activity/stream/{stream_key} so ActivitySmith can manage start, update, rotation, and end state for you. This endpoint remains supported for existing integrations and advanced lifecycle control. Updates an existing Live Activity. If the per-activity token is not registered yet, the update is queued. Supports segmented_progress, progress, metrics, stats, alert, and timer activity types. For segmented_progress activities, you can increase or decrease number_of_steps here as the workflow changes. For timer activities, send duration_seconds only when you want to reset the timer window; omit it to keep the current timer running. Use secondary_action for a second button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities only.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->liveActivities->update([
    'activity_id' => 'pLAr-Hnq9ZFW4sxlk43Lhbuok4GLh7UW',
    'content_state' => [
        'title' => 'Nightly database backup',
        'subtitle' => 'upload archive',
        'number_of_steps' => 3,
        'current_step' => 2,
    ],
]);

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

activitysmith.live_activities.update(
  activity_id: "pLAr-Hnq9ZFW4sxlk43Lhbuok4GLh7UW",
  content_state: {
    title: "Nightly database backup",
    subtitle: "upload archive",
    number_of_steps: 3,
    current_step: 2
  }
)

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

403.description: Forbidden (activity not owned by this API key account)

value.message: This API key cannot update this Live Activity
Too many requests, please try again later.

post.summary: End a Live Activity (legacy manual lifecycle)
post.description: Legacy manual lifecycle endpoint. For new integrations, use DELETE /live-activity/stream/{stream_key} to end a managed Live Activity stream. This endpoint remains supported for existing integrations and advanced lifecycle control. Ends a Live Activity and archives its lifecycle. Supports segmented_progress, progress, metrics, stats, alert, and timer activity types. For segmented_progress activities, you can send the latest number_of_steps here if the workflow changed after start. Use secondary_action for a second button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities only.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->liveActivities->end([
    'activity_id' => 'pLAr-Hnq9ZFW4sxlk43Lhbuok4GLh7UW',
    'content_state' => [
        'title' => 'Nightly database backup',
        'subtitle' => 'verify restore',
        'number_of_steps' => 3,
        'current_step' => 3,
        'auto_dismiss_minutes' => 2,
    ],
]);

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

activitysmith.live_activities.end(
  activity_id: "pLAr-Hnq9ZFW4sxlk43Lhbuok4GLh7UW",
  content_state: {
    title: "Nightly database backup",
    subtitle: "verify restore",
    number_of_steps: 3,
    current_step: 3,
    auto_dismiss_minutes: 2
  }
)

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

value.message: This API key cannot end this Live Activity
Too many requests, please try again later.

post.description: Updates the latest value for a metric displayed in ActivitySmith widgets. Create the metric in the web app first, then update its value using the key.

x-codeSamples.label: Node
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import os
from activitysmith import ActivitySmith

activitysmith = ActivitySmith(api_key=os.environ["ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"])

activitysmith.metrics.update("deploy.success_rate", 99.9)

x-codeSamples.label: Go
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php

use ActivitySmith\ActivitySmith;

$activitysmith = new ActivitySmith($_ENV['ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY']);

$activitysmith->metrics->update('deploy.success_rate', 99.9);

x-codeSamples.label: Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require "activitysmith"

activitysmith = ActivitySmith::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY"))

activitysmith.metrics.update("deploy.success_rate", 99.9)

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://activitysmith.com/api/metrics/deploy.success_rate/value" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTIVITYSMITH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "value": 99.9 }'

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Metric key configured in the web app. Lowercase letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes are allowed.

value.error: value must be a finite number

value.message: Too many requests, please try again later.

apiKeyAuth.description: Required. Include `Authorization: Bearer ask_123456789` in every request. Replace `ask_123456789` with your API key.

ContentStateStart.description: Start payload requires title and type. For segmented_progress include number_of_steps and current_step. For progress include percentage or value with upper_limit. For metrics and stats include a non-empty metrics array. For alert include message. For timer include duration_seconds for countdowns, or set counts_down false without duration_seconds for an open-ended elapsed timer. Optional icon is supported by all Live Activity types. Optional badge is supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress. For segmented_progress, number_of_steps is not locked and can be changed in later update or end calls.

number_of_steps.description: Total number of steps. Use for type=segmented_progress. This value can be increased or decreased later when updating or ending the same activity.

current_step.description: Current completed step count. Use for type=segmented_progress. Set 0 when the activity has started but no segment is complete yet. Must be less than or equal to number_of_steps.

percentage.description: Progress percentage (0–100). Use for type=progress. Takes precedence over value/upper_limit if both are provided.

value.description: Current progress value. Use with upper_limit for type=progress.

upper_limit.description: Maximum progress value. Use with value for type=progress.

duration_seconds.description: Timer duration in seconds. For type=timer countdowns, required on start when counts_down is true or omitted.

counts_down.description: Use with type=timer. When true or omitted, the timer counts down from duration_seconds. Set false for an elapsed timer; omit duration_seconds for an open-ended elapsed timer.

is_running.description: Use with type=timer. Defaults to true. Set false to pause/freeze via API; set true on a paused timer to resume.

icon.description: Optional SF Symbol icon. Supported by alert, progress, segmented_progress, metrics, stats, and timer.

badge.description: Optional badge. Supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress.

color.description: Optional. Accent color for progress, segmented_progress, metrics, and timer Live Activities. For Alert Live Activities, this tints action and secondary_action buttons when included.

step_color.description: Optional. Overrides color for the current step. Only applies to type=segmented_progress.

step_colors.description: Optional. Colors for completed steps. When used with segmented_progress, the array length should match current_step.

ContentStateUpdate.description: Update payload requires title. For segmented_progress include current_step and optionally number_of_steps. For progress include percentage or value with upper_limit. For metrics and stats include a non-empty metrics array. For alert include message. For timer, omit duration_seconds to preserve the current timer window or send duration_seconds to reset the timer from the update request time. Optional icon is supported by all Live Activity types. Optional badge is supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress. Type is optional when updating an existing activity. You can increase or decrease number_of_steps during updates.

number_of_steps.description: Total number of steps. Use for type=segmented_progress. Optional on update, and safe to change if the workflow gains or loses steps.

current_step.description: Current completed step count. Use for type=segmented_progress. Set 0 when no segment is complete yet. Must be less than or equal to number_of_steps when number_of_steps is provided.

percentage.description: Progress percentage (0–100). Use for type=progress. Takes precedence over value/upper_limit if both are provided.

value.description: Current progress value. Use with upper_limit for type=progress.

upper_limit.description: Maximum progress value. Use with value for type=progress.

duration_seconds.description: Timer duration in seconds. For type=timer, sending duration_seconds resets the timer window from the update request time; omit it to preserve the existing timer window.

counts_down.description: Use with type=timer. When true or omitted, the timer counts down from duration_seconds. Set false for an elapsed timer; omit duration_seconds for an open-ended elapsed timer.

is_running.description: Use with type=timer. Defaults to true. Set false to pause/freeze via API; set true on a paused timer to resume.

icon.description: Optional SF Symbol icon. Supported by alert, progress, segmented_progress, metrics, stats, and timer.

badge.description: Optional badge. Supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress.

type.description: Optional. When omitted, the API uses the existing Live Activity type.

color.description: Optional. Accent color for progress, segmented_progress, metrics, and timer Live Activities. For Alert Live Activities, this tints action and secondary_action buttons when included.

step_color.description: Optional. Overrides color for the current step. Only applies to type=segmented_progress.

step_colors.description: Optional. Colors for completed steps. When used with segmented_progress, the array length should match current_step.

ContentStateEnd.description: End payload requires title. For segmented_progress include current_step and optionally number_of_steps. For progress include percentage or value with upper_limit. For metrics and stats include a non-empty metrics array. For alert include message. For timer, omit duration_seconds to preserve and freeze the latest timer state. Optional icon is supported by all Live Activity types. Optional badge is supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress. Type is optional when ending an existing activity. You can send an updated number_of_steps here if the workflow changed after start.

number_of_steps.description: Total number of steps. Use for type=segmented_progress. Optional on end, and safe to change if the final workflow used more or fewer steps than originally planned.

current_step.description: Current completed step count. Use for type=segmented_progress. Must be less than or equal to number_of_steps when number_of_steps is provided.

percentage.description: Progress percentage (0–100). Use for type=progress. Takes precedence over value/upper_limit if both are provided.

value.description: Current progress value. Use with upper_limit for type=progress.

upper_limit.description: Maximum progress value. Use with value for type=progress.

duration_seconds.description: Timer duration in seconds. For type=timer, omit duration_seconds on end to preserve and freeze the latest timer state.

counts_down.description: Use with type=timer. When true or omitted, the timer counts down from duration_seconds. Set false for an elapsed timer; omit duration_seconds for an open-ended elapsed timer.

is_running.description: Use with type=timer. Defaults to true. Set false to pause/freeze via API; set true on a paused timer to resume.

icon.description: Optional SF Symbol icon. Supported by alert, progress, segmented_progress, metrics, stats, and timer.

badge.description: Optional badge. Supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress.

type.description: Optional. When omitted, the API uses the existing Live Activity type.

color.description: Optional. Accent color for progress, segmented_progress, metrics, and timer Live Activities. For Alert Live Activities, this tints action and secondary_action buttons when included.

step_color.description: Optional. Overrides color for the current step. Only applies to type=segmented_progress.

step_colors.description: Optional. Colors for completed steps. When used with segmented_progress, the array length should match current_step.

auto_dismiss_minutes.description: Optional. Minutes before the ended Live Activity is dismissed. Default 3. Set 0 for immediate dismissal. iOS will dismiss ended Live Activities after ~4 hours max.

StreamContentState.description: Current state for a managed Live Activity stream. Include type on the first PUT, and whenever the stream may need to start a fresh activity. Supports segmented_progress, progress, metrics, stats, alert, and timer types. For timer, send duration_seconds to start or reset a bounded timer; omit duration_seconds on later updates to preserve the existing timer window.

current_step.description: Use for segmented_progress. Set 0 when no segment is complete yet. Must be less than or equal to number_of_steps when number_of_steps is provided.

percentage.description: Use for progress. Takes precedence over value/upper_limit if both are provided.

value.description: Current progress value. Use with upper_limit for progress.

upper_limit.description: Maximum progress value. Use with value for progress.

duration_seconds.description: Timer duration in seconds. For type=timer, send duration_seconds to start or reset the timer window; omit it on later stream updates to preserve the existing timer window.

counts_down.description: Use with type=timer. When true or omitted, the timer counts down from duration_seconds. Set false for an elapsed timer; omit duration_seconds for an open-ended elapsed timer.

is_running.description: Use with type=timer. Defaults to true. Set false to pause/freeze via API; set true on a paused timer to resume.

type.description: Required on the first PUT or whenever the stream cannot infer the current activity type.

color.description: Optional. Accent color for progress, segmented_progress, metrics, and timer Live Activities. For Alert Live Activities, this tints action and secondary_action buttons when included.

step_color.description: Optional. Overrides color for the current step. Only applies to segmented_progress.

step_colors.description: Optional. Colors for completed steps. When used with segmented_progress, the array length should match current_step.

metrics.description: Use for metrics and stats activities.

icon.description: Optional SF Symbol icon. Supported by alert, progress, segmented_progress, metrics, stats, and timer.

badge.description: Optional badge. Supported by alert, progress, and segmented_progress.

auto_dismiss_seconds.description: Optional. Seconds before the ended Live Activity is dismissed.

auto_dismiss_minutes.description: Optional. Minutes before the ended Live Activity is dismissed.

color.description: Optional per-metric accent color for metrics and stats activities.

LiveActivityAlertIcon.description: Optional SF Symbol icon for Live Activities.

channels.description: Channel slugs. When omitted, API key scope determines recipients.

title.description: Button title displayed in iOS expanded notification UI.

url.description: Action URL. For open_url, use an HTTPS URL or a shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=... URL that runs a specific iPhone Shortcut. For webhook, use an HTTPS URL called by the ActivitySmith backend.

method.description: Webhook HTTP method. Used only when type=webhook.

body.description: Optional webhook payload body. Used only when type=webhook.

LiveActivityAction.description: Optional action button shown in the Live Activity UI. Use action for the primary button, or secondary_action for a secondary button on alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities.

title.description: Button title displayed in the Live Activity UI.

url.description: Action URL. For open_url, use an HTTPS URL or a shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=... URL that runs a specific iPhone Shortcut. For webhook, use an HTTPS URL called by the ActivitySmith backend.

method.description: Webhook HTTP method. Used only when type=webhook.

body.description: Optional webhook payload body. Used only when type=webhook.

media.description: Optional HTTPS URL for an image, audio file, or video that users can preview or play when they expand the notification. If `redirection` is omitted, tapping the notification opens this URL. Cannot be combined with `actions`.

redirection.description: Optional HTTPS URL or shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=... URL opened when the user taps the notification body. Use shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=... to run a specific iPhone Shortcut that already exists on the user's device. Overrides the default tap target from `media` when both are provided.

actions.description: Optional interactive actions shown when users expand the notification. Cannot be combined with `media`.

LiveActivityStartRequest.description: Start a new Live Activity. The response includes activity_id for later update and end calls.

secondary_action.description: Optional secondary action button. Supported only for alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities. Uses the same open_url, shortcuts://, and webhook shapes as action.

LiveActivityStartResponse.description: Returned after a Live Activity starts. Save activity_id and use it for all later updates and for the final end call.

LiveActivityStreamRequest.description: Send the latest state for a managed Live Activity stream. channels is the streamlined form for stream targeting. target.channels is also accepted for compatibility. If both are provided, they must match.

secondary_action.description: Optional secondary action button. Supported only for alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities. Uses the same open_url, shortcuts://, and webhook shapes as action.

channels.description: Channel slugs. When omitted, API key scope determines recipients.

LiveActivityStreamPutResponse.description: Returned after a managed stream request is reconciled.

LiveActivityStreamDeleteRequest.description: Optional payload for ending a managed stream. When omitted, ActivitySmith ends the stream using the latest known state when possible.

secondary_action.description: Optional secondary action button. Supported only for alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities. Uses the same open_url, shortcuts://, and webhook shapes as action.

LiveActivityStreamDeleteResponse.description: Returned after a managed stream is ended and removed.

MetricValueUpdateRequest.description: Latest metric value to display in widgets.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO timestamp for when the metric value was measured. Defaults to the server receive time.

active.description: Current number of active Live Activities.

LiveActivityUpdateRequest.description: Update an existing Live Activity by activity_id.

secondary_action.description: Optional secondary action button. Supported only for alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities. Uses the same open_url, shortcuts://, and webhook shapes as action.

LiveActivityUpdateResponse.description: Returned after a Live Activity update is sent or queued.

LiveActivityEndRequest.description: End an existing Live Activity by activity_id.

secondary_action.description: Optional secondary action button. Supported only for alert, progress, and segmented_progress Live Activities. Uses the same open_url, shortcuts://, and webhook shapes as action.

LiveActivityEndResponse.description: Returned after a Live Activity end event is sent or queued.
